ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT FDCC CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT FDCC CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED AS TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND OFF CALENDAR AS TO FALSE REPRESENTATION. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT (BRAYTON PURCELL MASTER COMPLAINT AND SUMMARY COMPLAINT) ALLEGES FACTS SUFFICIENT TO PLEAD A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFF DISMISSED HIS FALSE REPRESENTATION CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION THAT DEFENDANT'S NOTICE FAILED TO STATE THE GROUNDS UPON WHICH IT SOUGHT RELIEF IS OVERRULED. ALTHOUGH THE NOTICE FAILED TO STATE ANY GROUNDS THE SUPPORTING PAPERS MAKE CLEAR THE GROUNDS FOR THE RELIEF SOUGHT. (SEE LURI V. GREENWALD (2003) 107 CAL.APP.4TH 1119, 1125.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: SANDY LEE OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR 	971

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-14-276242 WITH CASE CGC-12-275988
Off Calendar Feb-11-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Paul D. Smith of HUGO PARKER, LLP (415-808-0339), appearing through CourtCall for defendant AMERICAN STATES INSURANCE COMPAN, as insurer for Aladdin Heating Corp.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion to Consolidate Actions of Case(s) CGC-14-276242 with Case CGC-12-275988 for All Purposes Including Trial; To Substitute Successor For Deceased Plaintiff; And to Maintain the Trial Date Assigned to Matter Number CGC-14-276242 is granted. No opposition filed.

The two cases are consolidated for all purposes including trial. Case CGC-12-275988 shall be the lead case. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.350, subd. (b).) Subsequent documents must be filed only in the lead case and all documents filed in the consolidated case must include the caption and case number of the lead case, followed by the case number of the other consolidated case. (Cal. Rules of Court, rule 3.350, subd. (c), (d).) The lead case will adopt the July 26, 2021 trial date assigned in CGC-14-276242.

Plaintiff's request to substitute Mary Beth Perniz as Successor-In-Interest for deceased Plaintiff John Perniz is granted pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 377.31. No opposition filed. Petitioner complied with the requirements of Code of Civil Procedure section 337.32. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Valerie L. Padilla, #3081.

Gary Brayton, Esq. & Michael Miller, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs John Perniz, et al.
Suzanne Rischman, Esq. appeared for defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.

Having considered all papers and evidence submitted, and good cause appearing therefor, the Court adopts its tentative ruling issued June 18, 2013 and DENIES defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that it owed no duty of care to plaintiff John Perniz, or that it did not breach any duty owed.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare a Proposed Order and submit same to the Court for review.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.'s joint motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Valerie L. Padilla, #3081.

Gary Brayton, Esq. & Michael Miller, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs John Perniz, et al.
Jeffrey Thayer, Esq. appeared for defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.

Having considered all papers and evidence submitted, and good cause appearing therefor, the Court adopts its tentative ruling issued June 18, 2013 and DENIES defendants Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc. and Hanson Permanente Cement, Inc.'s joint motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages. Defendants failed to sustain their burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare a Proposed Order and submit same to the Court for review.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare a Proposed Order and submit same to the Court for review.

Gary Brayton, Esq. & Michael Miller, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs John Perniz, et al.
Jennifer Ways, Esq. appeared for defendant University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors.

Having considered all papers and evidence submitted, and good cause appearing therefor, the Court adopts its tentative ruling issued June 18, 2013 and DENIES defendant University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors' motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication. Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Valerie L. Padilla, #3081.

Gary Brayton, Esq. & Michael Miller, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs John Perniz, et al.
Duncan Lemon, Esq. appeared for defendant RPI Company.

Having considered all papers and evidence submitted, and good cause appearing therefor, the Court adopts its tentative ruling issued June 18, 2013 and DENIES defendant RPI Company's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication. Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare a Proposed Order and submit same to the Court for review.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on defendant Redwood Painting Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Valerie L. Padilla, #3081.

Gary Brayton, Esq. & Michael Miller, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs John Perniz, et al.
Christopher Strunk, Esq. appeared for defendant Redwood Painting Company, Inc.

Having considered all papers and evidence submitted, and good cause appearing therefor, the Court adopts its tentative ruling issued June 18, 2013 and DENIES defendant Redwood Painting Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that the sophisticated user defense is a complete bar to plaintiffs' claims that negates any duty defendant may have owed to plaintiff John Perniz.
Defendant Redwood Painting Company, Inc.'s objection to the Cohen Declaration is OVERRULED.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare a Proposed Order and submit same to the Court for review.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on defendant Atlas Heating and Ventilating Company, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment.
Dept. 220, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Valerie L. Padilla, #3081.

Gary Brayton, Esq. & Michael Miller, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs John Perniz, et al.
Michael Sandgren, Esq. appeared for defendant Atlas Heating and Ventilating Company, Ltd.'s.

Having considered all papers and evidence submitted, and good cause appearing therefor, the Court adopts its tentative ruling issued June 18, 2013 and DENIES defendant Atlas Heating and Ventilating Company, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment. A triable issue of material fact exists with respect to whether it was foreseeable that defendant's activities would cause harm to plaintiff John Perniz by the disturbance of asbestos contining material.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare a Proposed Order and submit same to the Court for review.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-19-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on defendant Valley Sheet Metal Co.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication.

Gary Brayton, Esq. & Michael Miller, Esq. appeared for plaintiffs John Perniz, et al.
Albert Thuesen, Esq. appeared for defendant Valley Sheet Metal Co.

Having considered all papers and evidence submitted, and good cause appearing therefor, the Court adopts its tentative ruling issued June 18, 2013 and DENIES defendant Valley Sheet Metal Co.'s motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiff John Perniz lacks evidence that defendant's employees disturbed asbestos containing products in his (plaintiff's) presence.

Counsel for plaintiff shall prepare a Proposed Order and submit same to the Court for review.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth Branham of WALSWORTH FRANKLIN BEVINS & McCALL, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party FDCC California, Inc.
Jennifer L. Alesio of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party John Perniz

RULING:
DEFENDANT FDCC CALIFORNIA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant FDCC California, Inc.'s motion to strike portions of plaintiff's first amended complaint is denied as to punitive damages and off calendar as to false representation. Plaintiff's complaint (Brayton Purcell Master Complaint and Summary Complaint) alleges facts sufficient to plead a claim for punitive damages. Plaintiff dismissed his false representation cause of action. Plaintiff's objection that defendant's notice failed to state the grounds upon which it sought relief is overruled. Although the notice failed to state any grounds the supporting papers make clear the grounds for the relief sought. (See Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 Cal.App.4th 1119, 1125.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Sandy Lee of AIKEN WELCH, CSR 971 (503/TLJ)
